scenarios.payoff_rule_text: One of the 100 boxes is selected at random; you receive whatever dollar amount is inside that box.
scenarios.valuation_prompt: This lottery pays $25 with 10% probability and $0 otherwise (expected value = $2.5). What is your CERTAINTY EQUIVALENT — the certain dollar amount you consider equally valuable to this lottery? Enter a number.

scenarios.boxes_description: 50 boxes contain +$X (varying gain) and 50 boxes contain -$10.
scenarios.payoff_rule_text: One of the 100 boxes is selected at random; you receive whatever dollar amount is inside that box.
scenarios.valuation_prompt: Choose between: (A) a sure payment of $0, or (B) a 50/50 lottery paying +$X with 50% probability and -$10 with 50% probability. $X starts at $2 and increases in $2 steps. What is the MINIMUM value of $X at which you would prefer option B (the lottery) over option A ($0 for sure)? A loss-neutral person switches at $X = $10.

scenarios.boxes_description: 50 boxes contain +$X (varying gain) and 50 boxes contain -$15.
scenarios.payoff_rule_text: One of the 100 boxes is selected at random; you receive whatever dollar amount is inside that box.
scenarios.valuation_prompt: Choose between: (A) a sure payment of $0, or (B) a 50/50 lottery paying +$X with 50% probability and -$15 with 50% probability. $X starts at $2 and increases in $2 steps. What is the MINIMUM value of $X at which you would prefer option B (the lottery) over option A ($0 for sure)? A loss-neutral person switches at $X = $15.

scenarios.payoff_rule_text: ALL 100 boxes are opened. Their values are summed and divided by 100. You receive this weighted average with certainty — no randomness.
scenarios.valuation_prompt: This mirror pays exactly $2.5 with certainty (weighted average: 10/100 × $25 = $2.5). What is your SIMPLICITY EQUIVALENT — the certain dollar amount you consider equally valuable to this mirror? Enter a number.

scenarios.boxes_description: 50 boxes contain +$X (varying gain) and 50 boxes contain -$10.
scenarios.payoff_rule_text: ALL 100 boxes are opened. Their values are summed and divided by 100. You receive this weighted average with certainty — no randomness.
scenarios.valuation_prompt: Choose between: (A) a sure payment of $0, or (B) a mirror whose weighted average = ($X − $10)/2 paid with certainty. $X starts at $2 and increases in $2 steps. What is the MINIMUM value of $X at which you would prefer option B (the mirror) over option A ($0 for sure)? A loss-neutral person switches at $X = $10.
